changes.what: Added a red-border + error text under the meal name field, shown on blur or on a failed save attempt. Removed the name check from the save buttons' `disabled` condition and validate inside the handlers instead.
changes.why: The save buttons were disabled whenever the name was empty, so the button's onPress never fired and the user got no feedback on why they couldn't save.

changes.what: Replaced the fixed `pb-4` on the submit button with `paddingBottom: insets.bottom + 16` using useSafeAreaInsets.
changes.why: The portion-selection screen (opened from Foods.tsx) had no safe-area handling, so the submit button sat under the home indicator.

changes.what: Replaced the fixed `py-4` on the bottom action bar with `paddingBottom: insets.bottom + 16` using useSafeAreaInsets.
changes.why: This is a full-screen Modal (opened from Meals.tsx) with its own safe-area context, and its confirm button sat under the home indicator.
The container was `flex-1 items-center` with no `justify-content`, so the empty-state content (icon, text, 'צור מאכל חדש' button) was pinned to the top instead of centered.

changes.what: Added `justify-center` to the empty-state container, and changed the floating 'צור ארוחה חדשה' button offset from `bottom: bottom + 32` to `bottom: bottom` to match the equivalent fix in Foods.tsx.
changes.why: Same top-alignment bug as Foods.tsx, plus keeping the floating button offset consistent between the two tabs.

changes.what: Removed the redundant `pb-3` from the bottom button container.
changes.why: First pass at fixing an oversized gap under the onboarding step buttons; turned out to only be part of the cause (see UserDetails.tsx).

changes.what: Removed the redundant `pb-3` from the bottom button container.
Removed the redundant `pb-3` from the bottom button container.
Removed the redundant `pb-3` from the bottom button row.
Removed `paddingBottom: insets.bottom` from the KeyboardAvoidingView, and removed the now-unused `useSafeAreaInsets` import/variable.
changes.why: app/_layout.tsx already wraps the whole app in a GestureHandlerRootView with `paddingBottom: insets.bottom`, applied globally to every screen. UserDetails.tsx was adding the same inset a second time, which was the real cause of the oversized gap under the onboarding buttons.
